affiliation.name: Space Telescope Science Institute (STScI), Baltimore, MD

titles.title: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star: ET Wonders How You Are

descriptions.description: This poster presents two complementary studies of M dwarf magnetic activity from the REsearch Consortium On Nearby Stars (RECONS). The SURVEY component studies long-term stellar activity cycles in a volume-complete sample of 463 M dwarfs within 16.7 parsecs, uncovering ~20 confident stellar cycles in fully convective stars using 20+ years of differential VRI photometry from the SMARTS 0.9m at CTIO. The TWINS component studies magnetic activity differences between 36 pairs of nearly identical M dwarf wide binary companions compiled from Gaia DR2, using multi-wavelength observations (photometry, Hα spectroscopy via CHIRON, speckle imaging at SOAR, Chandra X-ray) to test assumptions of magnetic predictability in stars with identical masses and ages.

SURVEY: Differential VRI photometry with SMARTS 0.9m at CTIO (~7 mmag precision, 4-6 observations/year, >20 year baseline). Stellar cycle detection via Gaussian Process model (combining rotational modulation + long-term cycles) and Lomb-Scargle periodogram. TWINS: 36 M dwarf binary pairs from Gaia DR2 with photometric similarity <0.1 mag across BP, RP, J, H, Ks; mass difference <3%. Multi-wavelength activity tracers: SMARTS 0.9m photometry, CHIRON Hα spectroscopy, SOAR 4.1m speckle imaging, Chandra X-ray.
Presented at the American Astronomical Society meeting. File labeled AAC (AAS Conference) poster.

sections.sectionContent: M dwarfs comprise three-quarters of all stars (Henry et al. 2006; 2018). RECONS has spent over two decades studying nearby red dwarfs using the SMARTS 0.9m at CTIO (differential VRI photometry and astrometry, ~7 mmag precision, 4-6 obs/year, >20 year baseline).

SURVEY: Volume-complete sample of 463 M dwarfs within 16.7 parsecs south of 0° declination. TWINS: 36 common proper motion M dwarf twin wide binaries (72 total stars) from Gaia DR2, with photometric similarity <0.1 mag in BP/RP/J/H/Ks and mass differences <3%.

~20 confident stellar cycles uncovered in fully convective M dwarfs — the largest such collection. Four example cycles: GJ1061 (P=16.6 yrs, Δ=45 mmag), GJ234AB (P=6.1 yrs, Δ=25 mmag) via Gaussian Process; WT460AB (P=28.9 yrs, Δ=48.3 mmag), GJ358 (P=12.5 yrs, Δ=66.9 mmag) via Lomb-Scargle periodogram.

Testing assumption that twin stars with similar masses/ages evolve similarly in magnetic properties. Multi-wavelength observations: SMARTS 0.9m (variability/rotation), CHIRON (Hα), SOAR 4.1m (speckle), Chandra X-ray (4 pairs). Results show magnetic activity differences: significant Hα differences; photometric differences sustained over 7 years; ~3× Chandra X-ray flux difference in one pair.

imageCaptions.caption: SURVEY sample HR diagram (MV vs V-K) showing 463 M dwarfs within 16.7 parsecs color-coded by mass (0.60-0.30, 0.30-0.15, 0.15-0.075 M_sun).
TWINS sample CMD (MG vs BP-RP) from Gaia DR2, N=72 stars, with red lines connecting each binary pair (Δ≈0.35 M_sun separation).
Four example detected stellar cycles from SURVEY: GJ1061 and GJ234AB (Gaussian Process fits); WT460AB and GJ358 (Lomb-Scargle).

Observed magnetic activity differences in 4 TWINS pairs: Hα line strength comparisons (top); photometric activity differences over 7 years (bottom left); Chandra X-ray showing ~3× flux difference (bottom right).
